back_translation: When the seventh month came, the Israelites had settled in their towns, and the people gathered together as one in Jerusalem.

Then Jeshua son of Jozadak with his fellow priests, and Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel with his associates, began to build the altar of the God of Israel to offer burnt offerings on it, as it is written in the Law of Moses the man of God.
They set up the altar on its former foundation, because they feared the peoples of those lands, and they offered on it burnt offerings to the LORD, both morning and evening burnt offerings.
They celebrated the Feast of Tabernacles as it is written, and offered the daily burnt offerings by the number prescribed for each day.
After that they offered the regular burnt offerings, and those for the New Moons and for all the appointed sacred feasts of the LORD, as well as the freewill offerings brought to the LORD.
From the first day of the seventh month they began to offer burnt offerings to the LORD, although the foundation of the temple of the LORD had not yet been laid.

They gave money to the masons and carpenters, and food, drink, and oil to the people of Sidon and Tyre to bring cedar logs from Lebanon by sea to Joppa, according to the authorization that Cyrus king of Persia had given them.

In the second month of the second year after they came to the house of God in Jerusalem, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, Jeshua son of Jozadak, and the rest of their associates—the priests and the Levites—and all who had returned from captivity to Jerusalem began the work, and they appointed Levites twenty years old and upward to supervise the work of the house of the LORD.

So Jeshua with his sons and brothers, Kadmiel with his sons (the descendants of Yehudah), together with the sons of Henadad and their sons and brothers—all Levites—joined to supervise the workers in the house of God.

When the builders had laid the foundation of the temple of the LORD, the priests in their apparel stood with trumpets, and the Levites the sons of Asaph with cymbals, to praise the LORD as David king of Israel had commanded.

They sang responsively in praise and thanksgiving to the LORD: “For he is good, for his steadfast love toward Israel endures forever.” Then all the people shouted with a great shout, praising the LORD, because the foundation of the house of the LORD had been laid.
But many of the priests, Levites, and older family heads who had seen the first house wept loudly when they saw the foundation of this house laid before their eyes, while many others shouted for joy.
The people could not distinguish the sound of the joyful shout from the sound of the weeping, because the people shouted so loudly, and the sound was heard far away.
